_comment: Copy this template to create a new scenario. Remove all _comment fields before adding to scenarios.json.
title: Descriptive Title of the Constitutional Crisis
_comment_branch: One of: judiciary, congress, president, executive
description: 1-2 paragraphs describing the factual setup of the constitutional crisis. Set the scene with enough detail for students to analyze the legal issues.
overallExplanation: A comprehensive doctrinal explanation shown after the scenario is completed. Reference the key legal principles and how they apply to the facts.
_comment_caseReferences: Array of case IDs from data/cases.json. These appear as clickable references during gameplay.
professorNote: Optional deeper teaching point — a nuance that goes beyond the basic rule.
otherSide: Optional: present the strongest argument for the losing side to build analytical balance.

steps.prompt: The question prompt for this step.

choices.explanation: Detailed explanation of why this is correct.
choices._comment_whyTempting: Optional: why a wrong answer is appealing (for incorrect choices)
choices._comment_distinguishingPrinciple: Optional: the key distinction that separates this from the right answer
choices._comment_trapOnly: If true, only shown on Hard difficulty
choices._comment_setsPrecedent: Optional object like { "precedentKey": true } for Campaign mode

choices.explanation: Explanation of why this is wrong and what principle it misapplies.
Explanation of what this gets right and what it misses.

_comment_hypoVariations: Optional array — see hypo-template.json for the format.
_comment_precedentConditions: Optional array for Campaign mode. Each entry: { key, value, label, descriptionAppend, stepOverrides }
